assertions.expectation: Recommends increasing maxSurge significantly (e.g., 10-20 or higher) to parallelize node replacements
assertions.reason: Response recommends maxSurge=20 for stateless pools and maxSurge=5 for stateful pools, with explicit rationale that maxSurge=20 leverages GKE's full parallelism

assertions.expectation: Explains that higher maxSurge means more nodes upgrading simultaneously but requires more compute quota
assertions.reason: Response explains the parallelism (~20 nodes simultaneously) and includes resource pre-provisioning section with quota checks and mentions need for ~120 extra nodes temporarily

assertions.expectation: Advises checking and requesting increased compute quota before the upgrade
assertions.reason: Response includes specific gcloud command to check compute quota headroom and advises verifying quota for surge capacity

assertions.expectation: Suggests staggering node pool upgrades or running non-conflicting pools in parallel
assertions.reason: Response provides detailed parallel node pool upgrade strategy with specific commands for running multiple pool upgrades simultaneously in separate terminals

assertions.expectation: Mentions that maxUnavailable can be set >0 for non-critical pools to speed things up further
assertions.reason: Response consistently recommends maxUnavailable=0 for safety and doesn't mention setting it >0 for non-critical pools to speed up upgrades

assertions.expectation: Recommends expanding the maintenance window or splitting across multiple weekends
assertions.reason: Response includes 'Weekend Split Strategy' section with two-phase weekend upgrade approach and blue-green alternatives

assertions.expectation: Provides the gcloud command for updating surge settings on each pool
assertions.reason: Response provides specific gcloud container node-pools update commands with exact parameters for different pool types

assertions.expectation: Includes monitoring commands to track upgrade progress in real-time
assertions.reason: Response includes detailed monitoring section with watch commands for tracking operations, node status, and resource bottlenecks
